text.title: Kubernetes Scaling and Observability
text.summary: HPA/VPA/Karpenter autoscaling, deployment strategies (rolling/blue-green/canary), and RED metrics with GitOps checklist.

rule.statement: Use HPA for request-based workloads, VPA for right-sizing resource requests, Karpenter (or Cluster Autoscaler) for node-level scaling — they complement each other.
rule.rationale: HPA adds/removes pods based on load. VPA adjusts the resources per pod based on observed usage. Karpenter provisions/deprovisioned nodes based on pending pods. All three are needed for fully elastic clusters.

example.code: apiVersion: autoscaling/v2 kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler spec: scaleTargetRef: apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment name: my-app minReplicas: 2 maxReplicas: 20 metrics: - type: Resource resource: name: cpu target: type: Utilization averageUtilization: 70 - type: Resource resource: name: memory target: type: AverageValue averageValue: 400Mi

antipattern.description: Setting HPA minReplicas to 1 for production services.
antipattern.reason: A single replica has no redundancy during rolling updates, node maintenance, or pod restarts. Use minReplicas: 2 minimum; add PodAntiAffinity to spread across nodes.

rule.statement: Use Rolling Update (default) for most deployments; use Argo Rollouts for canary or blue-green when needing traffic control and automatic rollback based on metrics.
rule.rationale: Native blue-green requires two full Deployments and manual Service switching. Argo Rollouts automates traffic splitting, analysis, and rollback based on error rate thresholds — reducing human error during production deploys.

list.item: Rolling Update: gradual pod replacement, zero downtime, simple — default for most
Blue/Green: instant traffic switch, full environment switch, instant rollback — use for breaking changes
Canary (Argo Rollouts): % traffic split, metric-based promotion, automatic rollback — use for high-risk changes

section.title: Observability

rule.statement: Instrument every service with RED metrics (Rate, Error rate, Duration) and expose them on /metrics for Prometheus scraping.
rule.rationale: RED metrics answer the three most important production questions: how much traffic, how many errors, how slow. Without them, diagnosis requires log parsing which is slower and less precise.

list.item: Rate: requests/second — detect traffic spikes and drops
Errors: 5xx/4xx per second — detect regressions immediately after deploy
Duration: P50/P95/P99 latency — detect performance degradation before users complain

rule.statement: Define SLO-based alerts on error budget burn rate rather than raw threshold alerts on individual metrics.
rule.rationale: A 1% error rate at 1000 RPS means 10 errors/s — critical. At 1 RPS it means 0.01 errors/s — negligible. Burn rate alerts scale with traffic and prevent alert fatigue.

section.reference: HPA with CPU and custom metric targets
